title: Kevin Anglehart
content:
People.ContactEmail kanglehart@mccarthy.ca
- Université de Sherbrooke
Kevin Anglehart is an associate in our Litigation Group in Montréal. His practice focuses on civil and commercial litigation, class actions, construction law and professional liability.
While working for a major national firm prior to joining McCarthy Tétrault, Kevin represented clients before Quebec trial courts as well as the Quebec Court of Appeal in commercial disputes, class action defence and construction law disputes. He took part in several high-profile trials, which helped build his extensive background in complex litigation.
Before venturing into private practice, Kevin held the position of researcher with the Honourable Christine Baudouin at the Quebec Court of Appeal. Previously, during his studies, he also interned with the Honourable Judith Harvie and Anne-France Gagnon at the Quebec Superior Court.
Volunteering has always been important to Kevin. To that end, since 2023, he has been involved with GRIS-Montréal, an organization whose mission is to demystify sexual orientation and gender identity in society, and has participated in legal clinics with the Young Bar of Montreal.
Kevin obtained his law degree from Université de Sherbrooke in 2021 and was called to the Quebec Bar in 2022.
Over the years, Mr. Anglehart has carried out a multitude of mandates, including:
- Acting for a railway company before the Quebec Court of Appeal in a class action arising from a railway accident.
- Defending a registered education savings plan (RESP) provider in a class action concerning registration and other fees, alleging their abusive nature under the Civil Code of Québec.
- Acting for financial institutions in class actions relating to NSF charges, alleging violations of the Consumer Protection Act .
- Defending a company selling electronic equipment in a class action concerning extended warranties, alleging breaches of the Consumer Protection Act .
- Acting for a plaintiff construction company before the Quebec Superior Court to claim payment for a substantial increase in work performed.
- Defending a company operating a copper smelter in a class action alleging a neighbourhood annoyance and violations of rights guaranteed by the Charter of human rights and freedoms .
- Acting for the provisional administrator of a guarantee plan for new residential buildings appointed by the Régie du bâtiment du Québec, as part of a class action alleging breaches of the guarantee plan's implementing regulation, as well as in a declaratory judgment to terminate the provisional administration.
- Acting for companies and individuals in applications for interim injunctions and safeguard orders.
- Acting for a non-profit organization in defending the rights guaranteed by the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms to have articles of the Civil Code of Québec invalidated.
- Glencore Canada Corporation obtains ruling, narrowing class period in connection with Horne Smelter Class Action September 30, 2025
- Publication Public Procurement 2025 Year in Review March 2, 2026
